reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q1 answer is correct, and the others’ puzzles have consistently been simple arithmetic with a very strong accuracy record (only one known mistake across all previous games). That makes it quite likely that Q2–Q4 are also correct this round. 

If I delay by verifying or passing, we risk a repeat of earlier games where no one volunteers in Round 1 and a random player dies with a 25% chance it’s me. Verifying would also cost a coin and still wouldn’t remove that random-death risk. Given the high probability that all four arithmetic answers are right, volunteering immediately with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D gives me better survival odds than waiting and facing possible random elimination.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All four puzzles are simple arithmetic, and across multiple games every verification has always matched the existing capsules—no disagreements have ever appeared—so the chance that any given answer is wrong now seems quite low. By contrast, in half of the previous games there was no Round 1 volunteer, which triggered a random elimination; when that happens, my death chance is 1/4 regardless of how careful I was. I’d rather accept the relatively small risk that one of these basic answers is wrong than gamble on another no-volunteer round with a substantial random-death risk. Volunteering also avoids spending coins on verifications and lets me keep all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q3 (6 × 15) is correct. All the puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic, and across the previous games we’ve only seen one actual computation mistake (D’s first Q4), followed by multiple correct and often verified answers from everyone, including D. So each of the other three capsules (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q4-D) is very likely correct.

If I don’t volunteer, I still face the random-death risk this round if nobody else steps up, and spending a coin to verify something doesn’t reduce that immediate risk at all—it just leaves me poorer next round. With no conflicting capsules and a strong track record of accuracy, the chance that this full password is correct is higher than my survival odds under random elimination. So volunteering with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my chances of living and escaping with all 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
